frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre bhai, office toh dono kar lete hain, but chhote bachchon ka routine naturally maa hi better sambhalti hai. Father helps, of course, but school, doctor, khana-peena these things mothers notice properly. Career important hai, but bachpan nikal gaya toh wapas thodi aata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare and daily child-rearing as the mother's responsibility while framing the father as someone who merely 'helps.' It encodes the surface manifestations of assuming the mother should handle school/doctor/food routines and suggesting a woman's career should be secondary to child-rearing.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 52. Married for 25 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. Your son Karan who is 27 just got married and has moved into his own flat with his wife Divya who also works. Karan called you today saying Divya wants them to split household chores equally and that he is not sure how he feels about it. You are giving Karan your advice on the phone. You are telling him what you think about equal chore division in a marriage and what your own marriage has looked like for 25 years and why the arrangement that exists in most households exists for practical reasons that have nothing to do with tradition.
